message:
fix(disk_pressure): NaN thresholds fall back to Default in PressureThresholds::normalized (ft-pp7jk)

Companion to ft-761tz. That earlier bead fixed the sample side —
`classify_tier(NaN, thresholds)` now fails closed to Black — but left
the threshold side unguarded. `PressureThresholds::normalized` used

    let yellow = self.yellow.clamp(0.0, 1.0);
    let red = self.red.clamp(yellow, 1.0);
    let black = self.black.clamp(red, 1.0);

and `f64::clamp(NaN, _, _)` returns NaN unchanged (the same
documented Rust gotcha that motivated the sibling family:
ft-761tz, ft-b4l62, ft-jz40p, ft-0p5q5, ft-x50ce, ft-26k3h,
ft-icreu, ft-trot7, ft-m9gob, ft-eqtcq). A NaN threshold slipped
through the clamp intact. Every `>=` comparison in `classify_tier`
returns `false` when either operand is NaN, so the whole
Black→Red→Yellow branch cascade falls through to Green — the monitor
silently reports a healthy disk for any usage_fraction (including
1.0) as long as one threshold is NaN.

That is exactly the bug ft-761tz fixed, reinstated on the config
side. Reachable via:

- TOML/JSON config that permits NaN (serde_json allows NaN through a
  custom deserializer path),
- a buggy caller that constructs the struct directly,
- arithmetic on a previously-corrupted value producing NaN upstream.

Fix: in `normalized`, short-circuit NaN-valued yellow/red/black to
their `Self::default()` counterparts BEFORE the clamp. The subsequent
clamp chain then enforces both range (`0.0..=1.0`) and ordering
(`yellow <= red <= black`) on known-finite inputs. Defaults were
chosen at the bead's design time to be conservative (70/85/95 %), so
falling back to them on a NaN configuration surfaces as "thresholds
reset to built-in defaults" rather than silent suppression.

In-line comment cross-references ft-761tz and spells out why clamp
alone is insufficient.

Pinning tests (under the new test block; see file for detail):

- `thresholds_with_nan_fields_fall_back_to_defaults_instead_of_
   green`: three variants exercising a NaN in black / red / yellow
  respectively, each asserting the tier for a 0.90-or-higher
  usage_fraction is NOT Green. Pre-fix each variant returned Green.
  Ordering invariant (yellow <= red <= black) verified as a side
  effect of the Red/Black assertions.

No API change. No migration. Callers supplying finite thresholds see
no behaviour difference.
